notable_pick_trades: Traded T'Vondre Sweat to NYJ for Jermaine Johnson (straight player swap, no picks). Traded 7th-round pick 218 to DAL for Solomon Thomas (received pick 225). Hold two 5th-rounders and two 6th-rounders via prior trades.
roster_context: Year 2 of full rebuild under new GM Mike Borgonzi. New HC Robert Saleh (hired Jan 2026) with OC Brian Daboll. Went 3-14 in 2025 with sophomore QB Cam Ward (took a league-worst 55 sacks). Titans spent aggressively in free agency (~$300M in contracts on Day 1), primarily restocking the secondary and adding veteran pieces with Saleh/Daboll connections. Still have critical holes at EDGE, WR1, IOL, and LB heading into the draft. Pick #4 overall gives them a shot at elite defensive talent.

needs.explanation: The Titans' most glaring need. Lost Arden Key (signed with Colts), Jihad Ward (FA), and traded Dre'Mont Jones to BAL during the 2025 season. Acquired Jermaine Johnson from NYJ but he's coming off a torn Achilles (2024) and managed only 3 sacks in 2025. Jaylen Harrell showed late-season promise (4.5 sacks) but is a rotational piece, not a true starter. The Titans did not pursue a top-tier FA edge like Trey Hendrickson, strongly suggesting they intend to use the #4 pick on EDGE (Rueben Bain Jr., David Bailey, or Arvell Reese). Johnson is on an expiring contract ($13.4M 5th-year option) with no guaranteed extension.
needs.current_starter: Jermaine Johnson (trade from NYJ, on final year of contract)
needs.depth: Jaylen Harrell (rotational, 4.5 sacks in Year 2), Truman Jones, Oluwafemi Oladejo, Jacob Martin (FA signing), Malik Herring (FA signing)

needs.explanation: Signed Wan'Dale Robinson (4yr/$78M from NYG) to be the top target and restructured Calvin Ridley's contract to keep him for 2026. However, Ridley is 31, coming off a broken fibula (missed final 10 games of 2025 with only 17 catches/303 yards), and has underperformed his contract. Robinson is a slot receiver, not a true outside X. Rookie WRs Elic Ayomanor and Chimere Dike showed promise but neither is a proven WR1. Cam Ward still needs a true boundary alpha receiver to develop. Lost Van Jefferson in FA.
needs.current_starter: Calvin Ridley (restructured, 31 yrs old, injury concerns), Wan'Dale Robinson (FA signing, slot), Elic Ayomanor
needs.depth: Chimere Dike, Bryce Oliver, Mason Kinsey — all unproven young players
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 (pick #35) — this is a strong WR draft class

needs.explanation: Released Lloyd Cushenberry (C) and Kevin Zeitler (RG) is an unsigned free agent at age 36. Currently have no proven starters at center or right guard. Signed Austin Schlottmann (C, 1yr from NYG, career backup with 4 starts in 2025) and Cordell Volson (G, 1yr from CIN, missed all of 2025 with shoulder surgery). 2025 5th-round pick Jackson Slater could compete but is unproven. Peter Skoronski (LG) is elite, but both IOL spots beside him are wide open. Zeitler may still return but at 36, he's not a long-term answer regardless.
needs.current_starter: Austin Schlottmann (C, career backup) / Cordell Volson or Jackson Slater (RG)
needs.depth: Jackson Slater (2025 5th-rounder), Cordell Volson — very thin and unproven
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 for starting center; Round 4-5 for guard depth

needs.explanation: Cedric Gray had a strong 2025 season and is locked in as one starter. However, the Titans need someone to start alongside him. Cody Barton is not viewed as a long-term solution. The Titans did not sign a premier inside linebacker in free agency, which strongly suggests they're targeting one in the draft. Sonny Styles (Ohio State) is being discussed as a possible #4 overall pick. In Saleh's defense, the off-ball LB next to the MIKE is a critical role.
needs.depth: James Williams (2024 7th-rounder), Curtis Jacobs — lack impact talent
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 depending on board (Styles at #4 is possible)

needs.explanation: The safety room has been overhauled. Released Xavier Woods. Quandre Diggs (32, played in 2025 on 1-year deal) is a free agent and has not re-signed — status unclear. Amani Hooker was released during the 2025 season. Signed Tony Adams (1yr from NYJ, Saleh connection) and re-signed Jerrick Reed. Adams started 35 of 42 games over three years with the Jets but is unproven as a long-term starter. Marcus Harris (2025 6th-rounder) and Elijah Molden provide depth. The position lacks a true long-term answer at free safety.
needs.current_starter: Tony Adams (FA signing from NYJ), Elijah Molden / Marcus Harris
needs.depth: Jerrick Reed, Marcus Harris — adequate depth but no star

needs.explanation: Dan Moore Jr. (LT, signed to 4yr/$82M in 2025 FA) was serviceable but graded as a middle-of-the-pack tackle. JC Latham (RT, 2024 1st-round pick) had struggles in Year 1 and is still developing. Neither tackle is a proven above-average starter, but both have long leashes due to their investments. The Titans need developmental competition, not immediate starters. Ward took 55 sacks as a rookie — tackle play must improve.
needs.depth: Leroy Watson, John Ojukwu — limited NFL experience

needs.explanation: Traded T'Vondre Sweat (young run-stuffing NT) to Jets. Jeffery Simmons remains elite but needs a capable partner. Signed John Franklin-Myers (3yr/$63M from DEN, 7.5 sacks in 2025) as the new IDL starter opposite Simmons. Added Jordan Elliott (2yr/$8M from SF) and traded for Solomon Thomas (DAL) for depth. Lost Sebastian Joseph-Day in FA. The Franklin-Myers signing addresses the immediate need, but long-term depth behind Simmons is thin, especially after trading Sweat.
needs.depth: Jordan Elliott, Solomon Thomas, Malik Herring — veteran depth pieces

needs.explanation: Tony Pollard was retained rather than released for cap relief, signaling the Titans plan to keep him as RB1 in 2026. Pollard ran for 1,079 yards in 2024 but production slowed with an ankle injury. Tyjae Spears provides complementary depth. Kalel Mullings (2025 6th-rounder) adds to the room. Pollard's retention makes RB a lower priority, though Jeremiyah Love at #4 has been discussed as a possibility. Not a pressing need.
needs.depth: Tyjae Spears, Kalel Mullings — adequate for now
needs.draft_range_target: Day 3 or not at all unless Love falls and is BPA

free_agency_impact: Signed WR Wan'Dale Robinson (4yr/$78M from NYG) — addresses slot WR need but still lack true outside X
Signed CB Alontae Taylor (3yr/$60M from NO) and CB Cor'Dale Flott (3yr/$45M from NYG) — CB no longer a top need after massive investment ($105M on two CBs)
Released CB L'Jarius Sneed — saved $11.4M, ended disastrous 2-year tenure (12 games total)
Signed DL John Franklin-Myers (3yr/$63M from DEN) — fills IDL void left by T'Vondre Sweat trade, reunited with Saleh
Traded T'Vondre Sweat (NT) to NYJ for EDGE Jermaine Johnson — Johnson reunites with Saleh but coming off Achilles, only 3 sacks in 2025, on expiring deal
Restructured Calvin Ridley's contract — keeps 31-year-old WR for 2026 on reduced cap hit, removes immediate pressure to draft WR at #4 but future still uncertain
Released C Lloyd Cushenberry and S Xavier Woods — creates IOL and S needs
Kevin Zeitler (RG, 36) remains unsigned free agent — both sides evaluating; RG need critical if he does not return
Lost EDGE Arden Key to IND, TE Chig Okonkwo to WAS — did not pursue top FA edge rushers, strongly suggesting draft EDGE at #4
Signed QB Mitchell Trubisky (2yr, backup) — solidifies QB room behind Cam Ward, Will Levis likely gone
Signed TE Daniel Bellinger (3yr/$24M from NYG) and TE Tylen Granson — replaces Okonkwo with blocking-first TEs alongside Gunnar Helm
Signed S Tony Adams (1yr from NYJ), re-signed S Jerrick Reed — safety room retooled but lacks star talent
Signed G Cordell Volson (1yr from CIN) and C Austin Schlottmann (1yr from NYG) — IOL depth but neither is a proven starter
Did NOT sign a premier edge rusher or inside linebacker in FA — strongly points to defensive pick at #4 overall
scheme_fit_notes: Robert Saleh runs a 4-3 wide-zone defense with heavy Cover 3/Cover 1 principles. Edge rushers who can set the edge and win with speed-to-power are ideal (Saleh's defenses in NYJ and SF featured athletic 4-3 DEs). LBs must be rangy and athletic to cover in zone — Sonny Styles' size/speed profile fits perfectly. Franklin-Myers' versatility to play inside and out aligns with Saleh's scheme. CB additions (Taylor, Flott) are lengthy press corners who fit Saleh's man/zone hybrid coverage preferences. On offense, Daboll runs a play-action heavy scheme with RPOs and pre-snap motion; a true outside X receiver with contested-catch ability would unlock the offense alongside Robinson's slot work.
